issues.title: [Epic · Week 5] Production quality pass
issues.outcome: Complete the production quality pass outcome without pulling later platform work into the current gate.
scope: Own the phase dependency order.
Surface risks and scope cuts.
Close the phase only with evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Every child issue has a clear owner, milestone, and acceptance criteria.
Blocked work is represented using issue dependencies.
The phase gate has explicit evidence.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 5 · Quality] Accessibility, responsive, image, and Core Web Vitals pass
issues.outcome: Make the data-heavy, image-rich wiki comfortable across keyboard, assistive technology, desktop, and narrow viewports while meeting measured performance budgets.
scope: WCAG-oriented audit of core routes.
Responsive and text zoom behavior.
Responsive image pipeline and layout stability.
Bundle and Core Web Vitals baseline plus budgets.
Reduced-motion behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Core flows are keyboard-complete.
Automated accessibility checks have no critical violations.
Images have correct dimensions, alt decisions, formats, and lazy-loading behavior.
Performance report records target and observed values.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 5 · Quality] Static catalog PWA and offline update strategy
issues.outcome: Provide safe installability and useful offline access to the app shell and last successful catalog snapshot.
scope: Cache app shell and versioned static catalog.
Define update notification and stale-data behavior.
Avoid caching legal/takedown changes indefinitely.
Provide offline and storage-error UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Offline behavior is tested.
A new catalog version invalidates or migrates caches predictably.
The service worker cannot serve a removed asset forever.
PWA work does not block release if the P0 quality gate slips.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 5 · Security] Browser, content, dependency, and supply-chain hardening
issues.outcome: Harden the static web product and its update/deployment pipeline without adding enterprise infrastructure.
scope: Threat model static content, remote URLs, Markdown, images, service worker, and Actions.
Pin and minimize dependencies/actions.
Add dependency review, secret checks, content validation, and security policy.
Document hosting-header limitations and compensating controls.
acceptanceCriteria: No arbitrary HTML or unsafe URL protocol is accepted from catalog content.
Actions use least privilege.
Dependency and generated-artifact review steps are documented.
Security report lists residual risks.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 5 · Operations] Privacy-respecting runtime errors, feedback, and support diagnostics
issues.outcome: Decide how users report errors and how maintainers diagnose static-site failures without invasive tracking.
scope: Define error boundary and support bundle fields.
Decide whether telemetry is absent or opt-in.
Provide issue-report template with redaction guidance.
Define uptime/link/catalog freshness checks.
acceptanceCriteria: No personal data is collected by default.
Reports exclude local secrets and browsing history.
Users can identify catalog version and app version.
Operational checks are documented.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 5 · Quality] SEO, structured metadata, social previews, sitemap, and canonical URLs
issues.outcome: Make public wiki pages discoverable and shareable without sacrificing performance or legal accuracy.
scope: Per-route title/description/canonical metadata.
Open Graph and social image strategy.
Sitemap and robots policy.
Structured data only where semantically valid.
404 and GitHub Pages URL behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Metadata is deterministic from catalog records.
No missing/unlicensed image is referenced in social metadata.
Canonical URLs are correct for deployment host/base path.
Automated tests cover representative pages.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.
